mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 9.5% signals improved labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives equity risk premiums higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 95 signals manufacturing weakness that triggers risk-off sentiment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 95.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 55%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in consumer credit (RSAFS) below 350 billion indicates reduced household spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where RSAFS falls below 350 billion.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong employment growth that boosts corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 55%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 90 combined with rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.0% indicates stagflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of INDPRO < 90 and CPIAUCSL > 2.0% fails to produce negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of cases, or their joint predictive power drops below 0.3.
